title: Combination Sum with Unlimited Picks
description:
### Objective
Positive candidate numbers and a target, find all unique combinations where the chosen numbers sum to target. Each number may be chosen unlimited times. Return combinations in nondecreasing order.

### Function Contract
Implement `combination_sum(candidates: List[int], target: int)` in Python.
Do not change input/output semantics expected by the tests.

### Input and Output
- Input `candidates`: type `array` (example: `[2,3,6,7]`).
- Input `target`: type `number` (example: `7`).
- Return type: `array`.

### Requirements
- Return the exact expected output for every test case.
- Handle edge cases (empty input, duplicates, boundary values) without raising exceptions.
- Target complexity: time `O(S)`, space `O(T)`.

### Constraints
- 1 ≤ |candidates| ≤ 30
- 1 ≤ target ≤ 500
- Candidates positive
explanation:
Intuition
Backtracking with a start index allows reusing the same element by calling recursion with the same index. Sort to produce ordered paths and to prune when the partial sum exceeds target.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Sort candidates
2. Main Algorithm: DFS with (start, remain, path); loop i from start; if candidates[i] ≤ remain, choose and recurse with i, else break
3. Return Result: Add path when remain is zero

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: Pseudo-polynomial
- Space Complexity: O(target) recursion depth

Key Insights
- Sorting enables pruning early
- Allowing same index supports reuse

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to break after exceed loses pruning
- Allowing i+1 prevents reuse
hints: Sort then prune when x > remain
Pass same index to allow reuse
